prose:
**Oxygen frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO gap from the `frontier-orbitals` chemkit task. Method used: DFT tier `standard`, explicitly B3LYP/def2-TZVP, run with PySCF using exact four-center integrals (no `--density-fit` flag). Geometry: fixed user-supplied input coordinates from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz` (2 atoms); this was a single-point orbital calculation, not a geometry optimization. Environment: gas phase (`solvent: null`). Charge 0, multiplicity 3.

Headline HOMO–LUMO gap: **5.2880586 eV**.

Additional returned orbital values: HOMO = -8.8739616 eV; LUMO = -3.5859031 eV. Total electronic energy = -150.38872 Hartree = -4092.2855 eV. SCF converged: true, in 7 cycles. chemkit integrity.trustworthy verdict: true. Warnings: no warnings reported.

Reproducibility: chemkit returned CLI invocation: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 3 --accept-defaults /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpj8ctrt4u/live.json`. The result JSON path in that invocation is `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpj8ctrt4u/live.json`. The chemkit tool response did not include a live `.out` log path, so I cannot truthfully name one.
